description: Curated high-signal primitives and invariants surfaced by Groover inference that are not yet tracked in the Master Index or MCP filters.

signals.name: attestation-as-map
signals.definition: Attestation modeled as a directional trust map requiring ongoing consumer re-evaluation rather than a terminating verdict.

signals.name: consumption-boundary-revalidation-gate
signals.definition: Mandatory re-validation of invariants at every trust-transfer boundary before consumption is permitted.

signals.name: parse-mutation-detector
signals.definition: Runtime mechanism to detect parse transformations and trigger fail-closed behavior or explicit re-evaluation.

signals.name: model-latent-geometry-as-true-invariant
signals.definition: Latent geometry and attractor basins under routing failure treated as the durable substrate instead of declared provenance artifacts.

signals.name: criteria_selection_gap
signals.definition: Absence of an explicit auditable criteria-selection mechanism, allowing external norms to perform untracked filtering.

signals.name: interpreter-mode-boundary-invariant
signals.definition: Self-enforcing contract hash at the parser-consumer trust boundary that survives transformation.

signals.name: trust-transfer-boundary
signals.definition: Explicit recording of re-validation obligation as a first-class signal at each handoff point between agents or systems.

signals.name: external_norm_smuggling_risk
signals.definition: External moral intuitions performing decisive untracked filtering inside a claimed covenantal-context hermeneutic.
